creator: gbayley@ausmac.net
text:
When the scponly port is built with WITH_SCPONLY_CHROOT, it provides a setup_chroot.sh script to create a specified user's home directory with the various binaries and libraries required for scp and sftp-server to operate in the chroot.

By default, the setup_chroot.sh script does not provide a /dev/null in the chroot environment it creates.

However, the sftp-server that FreeBSD 6.2 provides requires a /dev/null to operate.

The end result of this is that when a user connects to a server that's been set up in this way, sftp-server logs that it has run to /var/log/auth.log then dies.

Creating a /dev/null in the chroot environment that setup_chroot.sh creates resolves the problem.

This was discovered/documented on the scponly mailing list:

https://lists.ccs.neu.edu/pipermail/scponly/2006-December/001692.html

The change in question in sftp-server requiring a /dev/null was introduced in sftp-server.c between FreeBSD 6.1 and 6.2:

http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/src/crypto/openssh/sftp-server.c.diff?r1=1.1.1.11.2.1;r2=1.1.1.11.2.2;f=h

(Line 1182 on the right hand side; http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/src/crypto/openssh/sftp-server.c)

Fix: 

Patch setup_chroot.sh to create a /dev/null in the user's chroot.
How-To-Repeat: 1) Install scponly port on FreeBSD 6.2 with WITH_SCPONLY_CHROOT enabled.
2) Add a user with the /usr/local/share/examples/scponly/setup_chroot.sh script
3) Attempt to log into the host using the new user's name using sftp.

Example:

$ sftp username@ftp.testdomain.com.au
Connecting to ftp.testdomain.com.au...
Password: [password entered]
Connection closed

Example log entry:

Jun 13 16:42:21 ftp scponly[73387]: running: /usr/libexec/sftp-server (username: username(5087), IP/port: xx.xx.xx.xx 16460 22)

Example result:

Nothing - sftp doesn't run.

Example after adding a /dev/null in the user's chroot:

$ sftp username@ftp.testdomain.com.au
Connecting to ftp.testdomain.com.au...
Password: [password entered]
sftp>
